Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]

=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353347]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Seven Shikobuchi''' (七シコブチ) is a collective term for the seven major shrines dedicated to the deity '''Shikobuchi-kami''', a faith unique to the {{ill|Azumi River|ja|安曇川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi River|1=Ado River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450763}} basin flowing through [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] and [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.
== The Seven Shikobuchi Shrines == === Kuta {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|思古渕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shikobuchi Jinja) === <gallery> File:1-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in {{ill|Kuta|ja|久多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuta|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], this shrine is home to the ''Hanagasa-odori'' dance, which is designated as an intangible cultural asset.
=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353347]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Seven Shikobuchi''' (七シコブチ) is a collective term for the seven major shrines dedicated to the deity '''Shikobuchi-kami''', a faith unique to the {{ill|Azumi River|ja|安曇川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi River|1=Ado River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450763}} basin flowing through [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] and [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.
== The Seven Shikobuchi Shrines == === Kuta {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|思古渕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shikobuchi Jinja) === <gallery> File:1-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in {{ill|Kuta|ja|久多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuta|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], this shrine is home to the ''Hanagasa-odori'' dance, which is designated as an intangible cultural asset.
=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.

Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.

; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353347]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Seven Shikobuchi''' (七シコブチ) is a collective term for the seven major shrines dedicated to the deity '''Shikobuchi-kami''', a faith unique to the {{ill|Azumi River|ja|安曇川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi River|1=Ado River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450763}} basin flowing through [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] and [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.
== The Seven Shikobuchi Shrines == === Kuta {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|思古渕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shikobuchi Jinja) === <gallery> File:1-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in {{ill|Kuta|ja|久多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuta|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], this shrine is home to the ''Hanagasa-odori'' dance, which is designated as an intangible cultural asset.
=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353347]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Seven Shikobuchi''' (七シコブチ) is a collective term for the seven major shrines dedicated to the deity '''Shikobuchi-kami''', a faith unique to the {{ill|Azumi River|ja|安曇川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi River|1=Ado River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450763}} basin flowing through [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] and [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.
== The Seven Shikobuchi Shrines == === Kuta {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|思古渕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shikobuchi Jinja) === <gallery> File:1-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in {{ill|Kuta|ja|久多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuta|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], this shrine is home to the ''Hanagasa-odori'' dance, which is designated as an intangible cultural asset.
=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. === Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11353347]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Seven Shikobuchi''' (七シコブチ) is a collective term for the seven major shrines dedicated to the deity '''Shikobuchi-kami''', a faith unique to the {{ill|Azumi River|ja|安曇川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi River|1=Ado River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450763}} basin flowing through [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] and [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.
== The Seven Shikobuchi Shrines == === Kuta {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|思古渕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shikobuchi Jinja) === <gallery> File:1-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in {{ill|Kuta|ja|久多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuta|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], this shrine is home to the ''Hanagasa-odori'' dance, which is designated as an intangible cultural asset.
=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.

It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.

; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")

== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:七シコブチ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353347]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Seven Shikobuchi''' (七シコブチ) is a collective term for the seven major shrines dedicated to the deity '''Shikobuchi-kami''', a faith unique to the {{ill|Azumi River|ja|安曇川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi River|1=Ado River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450763}} basin flowing through [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] and [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Legends of the ''Gawatarō'' ([[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]) remain in all seven shrines. The name "Shikobuchi" is written in various forms, including 思子淵, 志古渕, and 信興淵.
== The Seven Shikobuchi Shrines == === Kuta {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|思古渕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Shikobuchi Jinja) === <gallery> File:1-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in {{ill|Kuta|ja|久多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuta|1=Kuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369266}}, [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], this shrine is home to the ''Hanagasa-odori'' dance, which is designated as an intangible cultural asset.
=== Ogawa {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} === <gallery> File:2-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, front view </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Ogawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], Shiga Prefecture. A religious gathering called ''Shishibuchi Kō'' was held annually, where participants selected a host on a rotating basis, hung a sacred scroll inscribed with "Shishibuchi Shrine" in the alcove, made offerings, and prepared red rice for a communal meal. The shrine also possesses a copy of the ''Daihannya Sutra'', both designated as cultural assets.
=== Sakashita {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:3-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin, front view </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Sakashita, [[:en:Ōtsu|Ōtsu]], Shiga Prefecture. The shrine is a small sanctuary built atop a large boulder.
=== Bōmura {{ill|Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja|信興淵大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkōbuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:4-shicobuchi.JPG|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine main hall & auxiliary shrine (right side) </gallery> Located in Katsuragawa Bōmura, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Enshrined as an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja|葛川地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Jinushi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it is associated with the founding of {{ill|Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja|葛川明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragawa Myōō-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明王院 (大津市)|1=Myōō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512447}}.
=== Umenoki {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:5-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery> Located in Umenoki-chō, Ōtsu, Shiga Prefecture. Until recent times, its festival was held on the 7th day of the 10th lunar month.
=== Iwase {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|志子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === <gallery> File:6-shicobuchi.JPG|Shikobuchi Shrine, main hall </gallery> Located in Kutsuki Iwase, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture. Shikobuchi-kami is revered as the "God of Rafting," and an annual ''Shikobuchi Kō'' religious gathering is held.
=== Nakano {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|ja|思子淵神社 (高島市安曇川町中野)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine (Takashima, Azumi River Town, Nakano)|1=Shikobuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491514}} === <gallery> File:7-shicobuchi.JPG|Shishibuchi Shrine, approach path </gallery> Located in Azumi River Town, Nakano, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture.
== Other Shishibuchi Shrines == ; Kutsuki Miyamaebō {{ill|Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja|思子渕大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Enshrined within {{ill|Ninigi Shrine|ja|邇々杵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi Shrine|1=Ninigi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642701}}. Located between the main halls of Ninigi Shrine and {{ill|Kawachi Shrine|ja|河内神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Shrine|1=Kawachi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553441}}, three small shrines stand together. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Hosokawa {{ill|Shikobuchi Shrine|ja|シコブチ神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikobuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : An auxiliary shrine of the Hachiman Shrine, situated on a mountain slope along the right bank of the Azumi River. It is believed to have been relocated from another site.
; Katsuragawa Machii {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子渕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located north of the Machii village settlement along the left bank of the Katsuragawa River, on a mountain slope behind a temple.
; Ōhara Momoi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in {{ill|Ōhara, Kyoto|ja|大原 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhara, Kyoto|1=Ōhara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3594017}}, Momoi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. The shrine and a stage hall stand on a steep slope along the left bank of the Momoi River. Revered by forestry workers, its torii gate is constructed from unstripped {{ill|Kitayama cedar|ja|北山杉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama cedar|1=Kitayama Sugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401465}}. <gallery> File:思古淵神社.jpg|Shikobuchi Shrine </gallery>
; Ōhara Ōmi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ōmi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto. Uniquely situated at the source of the Ōmi River, with a river flowing between its approach path and main hall. : Destroyed by Typhoon No. 18 in September 2013, it was rebuilt in April 2018. A discovered ridge plaque revealed that the previous shrine was originally founded in the Muromachi period (1386) and rebuilt in the Edo period (1725). (''Asahi Shimbun Digital'', May 12, 2018: "Rebuilding the Shrine in Kyoto's Hidden Village: A New Beginning")
; Ōhara Ogoshi {{ill|Shishibuchi Shrine|ja|思子淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishibuchi Shrine|1=Shikobuchi Shrine (Takashima City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491513}} : Located in Ōhara Ogoshi-chō, Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, on a mountain slope along the left bank of the Ashibiyadani River. The shrine building has collapsed, leaving only the lanterns.
== The Gawatarō Legend == Long ago, Shikobuchi Myōjin and his child were rafting down the Azumi River from Tsuzukigahara in Katsuragawa Umenoki when their raft suddenly stopped. Upon investigating, they discovered that a large rock in Kanayama-buchi had blocked the raft's movement. At the same time, Shikobuchi Myōjin's child had vanished. Shocked, Myōjin plunged his pole into the water and searched the depths, finding a kappa hiding at the bottom with the child. Myōjin immediately captured the kappa, rescued his child, and scolded the kappa harshly. However, when they reached ''Aka-kabe'' in Nakano, the kappa reappeared and disrupted the raft again. Enraged, Myōjin warned the kappa, "From now on, if you ever interfere with someone wearing a ''suge no minokasa'' (straw raincoat) and ''gama no kyahan'' (rush leggings) while carrying a ''kobushi'' pole, you will face severe punishment!" It is believed that Shikobuchi Myōjin was originally a local land deity. == See Also == * [[:en:Kappa (folklore)|Kappa]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-butai.html Tracing Shiga’s Landscape Through Folktales: "Shikobuchi-san"] (Includes audio) ** [http://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/minwa/54/54-01.html Shikobuchi Shrine]
